question: The whole system in one picture
title: The whole system in one picture
summary: Browser pages talk to PHP, which reads and writes a SQLite database.

scenes.headline: Three layers. That is the whole shape.
scenes.body: There is no front-controller framework and no middleware stack. Routing is file-based and intentionally obvious.

scenes.label: System layers
scenes.description: Select a layer to hear what it does in plain language.

layers.title: Browser pages
layers.detail: Forms and lists in the browser gather what the operator types and show idea records after they are loaded.

layers.title: PHP application flow
layers.text: Validates requests, decides what to save, and loads data for display.
layers.detail: Entry scripts open SQLite, validate POSTs, write with prepared statements, redirect to safe GETs, and hand arrays to templates.

layers.title: SQLite database
layers.text: Stores the idea records between visits.
layers.detail: Each idea is a durable row with title, notes, status, and timestamps. Backups mean copying the database file.
